etymology.description: 彳(彳) (chì, "step; road") + 惪(惪) (dí, "virtue") -> virtuous path
etymology.interpretation: The character 德 (dé, "virtue") combines 彳(彳) (chì, "step; road"), indicating movement or progress, with 惪 (a variant which carries the meaning of "virtue"). This suggests a path of virtue or moral conduct.

examples.chinese: 儒家思想强调道德与文化(儒家思想強調道德與文化)。
examples.pinyin: Rújiā sīxiǎng qiángdiào dàodé yǔ wénhuà.

examples.chinese: 她的行为展现了高尚的品德(她的行為展現了高尚的品德)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de xíngwéi zhǎnxiànle gāoshàng de pǐndé.

examples.chinese: 在中国历史中，很多帝王以德治国(在中國歷史中，很多帝王以德治國)。
examples.pinyin: Zài Zhōngguó lìshǐ zhōng, hěnduō dìwáng yǐ dé zhìguó.
examples.english: In Chinese history, many emperors ruled the country with virtue.
